title: Size chunks to the query window
description: Choose hypertable chunk intervals so the working set spans a small number of chunks, balancing insert and query performance.
body: Use when creating a hypertable or diagnosing slow time-range queries. Do not leave the default chunk interval on high-ingest tables — too-large chunks bloat the working set, too-small ones explode chunk count and planning time. Actions: pick an interval so recent data (most-queried) fits ~1-2 chunks and a typical query touches a handful; inspect chunk count and sizes before tuning. Validation signals include query plans hitting few chunks and chunk size near the target memory budget. Risk boundary: changing chunk interval only affects new chunks — escalate if existing data needs re-chunking under load.
